description: Reproducible third_party registry for Gaussian Splatting / novel view synthesis experiments. Revisions are verified upstream HEADs as of 2026-07-07.

repos.name: Mip-Splatting
repos.notes: Anti-aliased 3DGS variant used by src/method_runners/run_mip_splatting_scene.py. Strong default for high-resolution drone imagery.

repos.name: 2D Gaussian Splatting
repos.notes: Surface-oriented Gaussian method used by src/method_runners/run_2dgs_scene.py; useful for thin BTS/tower structure detail.

repos.name: Official 3D Gaussian Splatting
repos.notes: Official 3DGS reference implementation. This repo currently uses the gsplat package for its supported 3DGS baseline to avoid CUDA extension conflicts.

repos.name: Scaffold-GS
repos.notes: Anchor/scaffold based Gaussian representation; candidate for quality-speed-memory tradeoffs. Requires a wrapper before benchmark automation can call it.

repos.name: Octree-GS
repos.notes: LOD/octree Gaussian variant for scalable rendering. Requires wrapper and memory profiling for this competition format.

repos.name: Hierarchical 3D Gaussians
repos.notes: Official-group hierarchical 3DGS for larger scenes. Candidate if public/private scenes become memory or scale limited.

repos.name: Gaussian Opacity Fields
repos.notes: Geometry/surface-oriented Gaussian method. Potentially relevant for tower structure consistency, but not wired into this pipeline yet.

repos.name: SuGaR
repos.notes: Surface-aligned Gaussian refinement/extraction. More useful for geometry quality than direct leaderboard rendering unless a wrapper is added.

repos.name: 3DGS-MCMC
repos.notes: MCMC-based densification/training variant. Candidate for better optimization under constrained iteration budgets.

repos.name: Gaussian Splatting Lightning
repos.notes: PyTorch Lightning framework that aggregates multiple 3DGS variants; useful as an experimentation harness, not currently wrapped.

repos.name: gsplat
repos.notes: Source for the gsplat package used by src/method_runners/run_3dgs_scene.py. Normal install remains pip package from pyproject.toml.
